org.bytedeco.onnxruntime

Class OrtEp

  • java.lang.Object
    • org.bytedeco.javacpp.Pointer
      • org.bytedeco.onnxruntime.OrtEp
  • All Implemented Interfaces:
    AutoCloseable

    @Properties(inherit=onnxruntime.class)
public class OrtEp
extends Pointer
    \brief The OrtEp struct provides functions to implement for an execution provider.
    Since:
    Version 1.22.

      • ort_version_supported

        @Cast(value="uint32_t")
public int ort_version_supported()
        \brief The ONNX Runtime version the execution provider was compiled with. Implementation should set to ORT_API_VERSION. ORT will use this to ensure it does not call functions that were not available when the library was compiled.
        Since:
        Version 1.22.

      • ort_version_supported

        public OrtEp ort_version_supported(int setter)

      • GetName

      • GetCapability

        public OrtStatus GetCapability(OrtEp this_ptr,
                               @Const
                               OrtGraph graph,
                               OrtEpGraphSupportInfo graph_support_info)
        \brief Get information about the nodes supported by the OrtEp instance. IMPORTANT: This is not the final version of this API function. This is currently experimental but will be stabilized by the ONNX Runtime 1.23 release.
        Parameters:
        this_ptr - [in] The OrtEp instance.
        graph - [in] The OrtGraph instance for which to populate node support. The OrtGraph could be a nested subgraph contained by a node (e.g., an If or Loop node). ONNX Runtime calls this function separately for each nested subgraph.
        graph_support_info - [inout] OrtEpGraphSupportInfo instance that the implementer must fill out in order to specify the supported nodes. \snippet{doc} snippets.dox OrtStatus Return Value
        Since:
        Version 1.23.

      • Compile

        public OrtStatus Compile(OrtEp this_ptr,
                         @Cast(value="const OrtGraph**")
                         PointerPointer graphs,
                         @Cast(value="const OrtNode**")
                         PointerPointer fused_nodes,
                         @Cast(value="size_t")
                         long count,
                         @Cast(value="OrtNodeComputeInfo**")
                         PointerPointer node_compute_infos,
                         @Cast(value="OrtNode**")
                         PointerPointer ep_context_nodes)
        \brief Compile OrtGraph instances assigned to the OrtEp. Implementer must set a OrtNodeComputeInfo instance for each OrtGraph in order to define its computation function. If the session is configured to generate a pre-compiled model, the execution provider must return EPContext nodes, as OrtNode instances, that ONNX Runtime uses to create a pre-compiled model, known as an "EPContext model". An EPContext model contains EPContext nodes. Each EPContext node encapsulates the pre-compiled binary data for a OrtGraph compiled for a specific execution provider. For more details about the EPContext design, refer to: \htmlonly EPContext design document. \endhtmlonly
        Parameters:
        this_ptr - [in] The OrtEp instance.
        graphs - [in] Array of count OrtGraph instances to compile. Each graph contains only the nodes for which the execution provider indicated support. Nested subgraphs contained by a node, such as an If or Loop, have separate OrtGraph instances.
        fused_nodes - [in] Array of count fused nodes that will replace the compiled graphs. Each fused node is an OrtNode initialized with the intended fused node name and input/output information.
        count - [in] The number of OrtGraph instances to compile.
        node_compute_infos - [out] Array of count OrtNodeComputeInfo instances that define each OrtGraph instance's computation function. The implementer allocates the OrtNodeComputeInfo instances. ORT calls ReleaseNodeComputeInfos() to release multiple instances in a batch.
        ep_context_nodes - [out] Output array of count OrtNode instances, each representing an EPContext node for a compiled OrtGraph. The execution provider must use OrtModelEditorApi::CreateNode to create the OrtNode instances. ONNX Runtime takes ownership of the OrtNode instances, so the execution provider must NOT call OrtApi::ReleaseNode. Should be ignored if the session is not configured to generate an EPContext model. \snippet{doc} snippets.dox OrtStatus Return Value \note Do NOT cache the provided OrtGraph instances in any of the OrtNodeComputeInfo functions because the graphs are only valid for the duration of the call to Compile. Any graph/node/input/output names that are needed by the OrtNodeComputeInfo functions must be copied and stored by the OrtEp. \note As of version 1.24, implementation of this function is optional if the EP does not compile nodes and uses a kernel registry instead.
        Since:
        Version 1.23.

      • Compile

        public OrtStatus Compile(OrtEp this_ptr,
                         @Const @ByPtrPtr
                         OrtGraph graphs,
                         @Const @ByPtrPtr
                         OrtNode fused_nodes,
                         @Cast(value="size_t")
                         long count,
                         @ByPtrPtr
                         OrtNodeComputeInfo node_compute_infos,
                         @ByPtrPtr
                         OrtNode ep_context_nodes)

      • ReleaseNodeComputeInfos

      • GetPreferredDataLayout

        public OrtStatus GetPreferredDataLayout(OrtEp this_ptr,
                                        @Cast(value="OrtEpDataLayout*")
                                        IntPointer preferred_data_layout)
        \brief Get the EP's preferred data layout. \note Implementation of this function is optional. If not implemented, ORT will assume that this EP prefers the data layout OrtEpDataLayout::NCHW.
        Parameters:
        this_ptr - [in] The OrtEp instance.
        preferred_data_layout - [out] The EP's preferred data layout. \snippet{doc} snippets.dox OrtStatus Return Value
        Since:
        Version 1.23.

      • GetPreferredDataLayout

        public OrtStatus GetPreferredDataLayout(OrtEp this_ptr,
                                        @Cast(value="OrtEpDataLayout*")
                                        IntBuffer preferred_data_layout)

      • GetPreferredDataLayout

        public OrtStatus GetPreferredDataLayout(OrtEp this_ptr,
                                        @Cast(value="OrtEpDataLayout*")
                                        int[] preferred_data_layout)

      • ShouldConvertDataLayoutForOp

        public OrtStatus ShouldConvertDataLayoutForOp(OrtEp this_ptr,
                                              @Cast(value="const char*")
                                              BytePointer domain,
                                              @Cast(value="const char*")
                                              BytePointer op_type,
                                              @Cast(value="OrtEpDataLayout")
                                              int target_data_layout,
                                              IntPointer should_convert)
        \brief Given an op with domain domain and type op_type, determine whether an associated node's data layout should be converted to target_data_layout. If the EP prefers a non-default data layout (see GetPreferredDataLayout()), this function will be called during layout transformation with target_data_layout set to the EP's preferred data layout. \note Implementation of this function is optional. If an EP prefers a non-default data layout, it may implement this to customize the specific op data layout preferences at a finer granularity.
        Parameters:
        this_ptr - [in] The OrtEp instance.
        domain - [in] The op domain. An empty string means the ONNX domain.
        op_type - [in] The op type.
        target_data_layout - [in] The target data layout.
        should_convert - [out] Whether the associated node's data layout should be converted to target_data_layout. If greater than 0, convert. If 0, don't convert. Otherwise, if less than 0, leave the decision to ORT. \snippet{doc} snippets.dox OrtStatus Return Value
        Since:
        Version 1.23.

      • ShouldConvertDataLayoutForOp

        public OrtStatus ShouldConvertDataLayoutForOp(OrtEp this_ptr,
                                              String domain,
                                              String op_type,
                                              @Cast(value="OrtEpDataLayout")
                                              int target_data_layout,
                                              IntBuffer should_convert)

      • SetDynamicOptions

        public OrtStatus SetDynamicOptions(OrtEp this_ptr,
                                   @Cast(value="const char*const*")
                                   PointerPointer option_keys,
                                   @Cast(value="const char*const*")
                                   PointerPointer option_values,
                                   @Cast(value="size_t")
                                   long num_options)
        \brief Set dynamic options on this EP. Dynamic options can be set by the user at any time after session creation with OrtApi::SetEpDynamicOptions().
        Parameters:
        this_ptr - [in] The OrtEp instance.
        option_keys - [in] The dynamic option keys.
        option_values - [in] The dynamic option values.
        num_options - [in] The number of dynamic options. \note Implementation of this function is optional. An EP should only implement this if it needs to handle any dynamic options. \snippet{doc} snippets.dox OrtStatus Return Value
        Since:
        Version 1.23.

      • SetDynamicOptions

        public OrtStatus SetDynamicOptions(OrtEp this_ptr,
                                   @Cast(value="const char*const*") @ByPtrPtr
                                   BytePointer option_keys,
                                   @Cast(value="const char*const*") @ByPtrPtr
                                   BytePointer option_values,
                                   @Cast(value="size_t")
                                   long num_options)

                                   ByteBuffer option_keys,

                                   ByteBuffer option_values,
                                   @Cast(value="size_t")

      • OnRunStart

        public OrtStatus OnRunStart(OrtEp this_ptr,
                            @Const
                            OrtRunOptions run_options)
        \brief Called by ORT to notify the EP of the start of a run.
        Parameters:
        this_ptr - [in] The OrtEp instance.
        run_options - [in] The run options for this run. \note Implementation of this function is optional. \snippet{doc} snippets.dox OrtStatus Return Value
        Since:
        Version 1.23.

      • OnRunEnd

        public OrtStatus OnRunEnd(OrtEp this_ptr,
                          @Const
                          OrtRunOptions run_options,
                          @Cast(value="bool")
                          boolean sync_stream)
        \brief Called by ORT to notify the EP of the end of a run.
        Parameters:
        this_ptr - [in] The OrtEp instance.
        run_options - [in] The run options for this run.
        sync_stream - [in] Whether any associated stream should be synchronized during this call. Only applicable if there is such a stream. \note Implementation of this function is optional. \snippet{doc} snippets.dox OrtStatus Return Value
        Since:
        Version 1.23.

      • CreateAllocator

        public OrtStatus CreateAllocator(OrtEp this_ptr,
                                 @Const
                                 OrtMemoryInfo memory_info,
                                 @Cast(value="OrtAllocator**")
                                 PointerPointer allocator)
        \brief Create an OrtAllocator for the given OrtMemoryInfo for an OrtSession. The OrtMemoryInfo instance will match one of the values set in the OrtEpDevice using EpDevice_AddAllocatorInfo. Any allocator specific options should be read from the session options. If nullptr OrtEpFactory::CreateAllocator will be used.
        Parameters:
        this_ptr - [in] The OrtEpFactory instance.
        memory_info - [in] The OrtMemoryInfo to create the allocator for. May be nullptr.
        allocator - [out] The created OrtAllocator instance. Set to nullptr if the default CPU allocator is used. \snippet{doc} snippets.dox OrtStatus Return Value
        Since:
        Version 1.23.

      • CreateAllocator

        public OrtStatus CreateAllocator(OrtEp this_ptr,
                                 @Const
                                 OrtMemoryInfo memory_info,
                                 @ByPtrPtr
                                 OrtAllocator allocator)

      • CreateSyncStreamForDevice

        public OrtStatus CreateSyncStreamForDevice(OrtEp this_ptr,
                                           @Const
                                           OrtMemoryDevice memory_device,
                                           @Cast(value="OrtSyncStreamImpl**")
                                           PointerPointer stream)
        \brief Create a synchronization stream for the given memory device for an OrtSession. This is used to create a synchronization stream for the execution provider and is used to synchronize operations on the device during model execution. Any stream specific options should be read from the session options. If nullptr OrtEpFactory::CreateSyncStreamForDevice will be used.
        Parameters:
        this_ptr - [in] The OrtEpFactory instance.
        memory_device - [in] The OrtMemoryDevice to create the synchronization stream for.
        stream - [out] The created OrtSyncStreamImpl instance. nullptr if the execution provider is not stream aware. \snippet{doc} snippets.dox OrtStatus Return Value
        Since:
        Version 1.23.

      • CreateSyncStreamForDevice

        public OrtStatus CreateSyncStreamForDevice(OrtEp this_ptr,
                                           @Const
                                           OrtMemoryDevice memory_device,
                                           @ByPtrPtr
                                           OrtSyncStreamImpl stream)

      • GetCompiledModelCompatibilityInfo

        public OrtEp.GetCompiledModelCompatibilityInfo_OrtEp_OrtGraph GetCompiledModelCompatibilityInfo()

      • GetCompiledModelCompatibilityInfo

        public OrtEp GetCompiledModelCompatibilityInfo(OrtEp.GetCompiledModelCompatibilityInfo_OrtEp_OrtGraph setter)

      • GetKernelRegistry

        public OrtStatus GetKernelRegistry(OrtEp this_ptr,
                                   @Cast(value="const OrtKernelRegistry**")
                                   PointerPointer kernel_registry)
        \brief Gets the execution provider's kernel registry, if any. A kernel registry contains kernel creation information for operator kernels supported by an EP.
        Parameters:
        this_ptr - [in] The OrtEp instance.
        kernel_registry - [out] Output parameter set to the EP's kernel registry, which must remain valid throughout the lifetime of the EP. Can be NULL if the EP doesn't use a kernel registry. \snippet{doc} snippets.dox OrtStatus Return Value \note Implementation of this function is optional. If set to NULL, ORT assumes the EP compiles nodes.
        Since:
        Version 1.24.

      • GetKernelRegistry

        public OrtStatus GetKernelRegistry(OrtEp this_ptr,
                                   @Const @ByPtrPtr
                                   OrtKernelRegistry kernel_registry)

      • IsConcurrentRunSupported

        public OrtStatus IsConcurrentRunSupported(OrtEp this_ptr,
                                          @Cast(value="bool*")
                                          BoolPointer is_supported)
        \brief Gets whether the execution provider supports concurrent run calls made on the session.
        Parameters:
        this_ptr - [in] The OrtEp instance.
        is_supported - [out] Whether concurrent runs are supported. \snippet{doc} snippets.dox OrtStatus Return Value \note Implementation of this function is optional and it may be set to NULL. If not implemented, ORT assumes that concurrent runs are supported.
        Since:
        Version 1.24.

      • IsConcurrentRunSupported

        public OrtStatus IsConcurrentRunSupported(OrtEp this_ptr,
                                          @Cast(value="bool*")
                                          boolean[] is_supported)
